commonIssues: Biennial: bolts to seed and turns bitter in its second year, so it is usually grown and replaced as an annual.
Slow, erratic germination (2 to 5 weeks); soaking seed up to 24 hours before sowing improves the low germination rate.
Container compost dries out quickly in warm weather; check moisture often and do not let it dry out completely.
Pests/diseases: carrot fly, aphids, slugs and snails (RHS); septoria leaf spot, stem rot, carrot weevil, flea beetles, leafhoppers (Missouri Botanical Garden).
Recommended container minimum (4 L) suits a single plant in a pot at least 25 cm (10 in) wide and deep; grow only one plant per 4 L, or use ~15 L for a cluster of about four plants.

source.citation: University of Minnesota Extension — Growing parsley in home gardens: full sun six to eight hours/day; water deeply at least once a week; fertilize once or twice per season with 5-10-5, container plants liquid-fed every 3 to 4 weeks; final spacing 10 to 12 inches; soil pH 6.0 to 7.0; germinates in 2 to 5 weeks; biennial that can handle light frosts.

The Old Farmer's Almanac — Parsley: full sun (6+ hours); best germination soil temperature ~70F (21C), germinates as low as 50F (10C); four plants per 14-inch / 4-gallon (~15 L) container; soak seeds overnight, germination 2 to 4 weeks.

Royal Horticultural Society — How to grow parsley: full sun or partial shade; keep moist but not waterlogged, container compost dries quickly; in-ground needs no feed, containers a balanced liquid feed every few weeks; sow in a pot at least 25 cm (10 in) wide and deep; biennial that dies down over winter; pests include slugs, snails, aphids, carrot fly.

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder — Petroselinum crispum: USDA hardiness zones 2 to 11; full sun to part shade; medium water, do not allow soils to dry out; 0.75 to 1.0 ft tall; biennial usually grown as an annual; performs best in cool summer climates; pests/diseases include septoria leaf spot, stem rot, carrot weevil, flea beetles, leafhoppers.

Confidence note — coldHardyMinC (-5C) and heatStressC (27C) are conservative estimates synthesized from MoBot hardiness zones 2-11 and 'best in cool summer climates' plus UMN/Almanac frost-tolerance and germination data; no single extension source states an exact leaf cold-kill or heat-stress threshold in Celsius. baseIntervalDays (3) and hotWeatherMultiplier (1.5) interpret UMN 'water deeply at least once a week' and RHS 'keep moist, dries quickly in warm weather' into the app's day-interval model.
